title: Pashupatinath Temple
tagline: Pashupatinath Temple — spiritual town of Madhya Pradesh

overview.short: Pashupatinath Temple at Mandsaur, also referred to as the Mandsaur Shiva temple, is a Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Shiva in Mandsaur, Madhya Pradesh, India.
overview.description: Pashupatinath Temple at Mandsaur, also referred to as the Mandsaur Shiva temple, is a Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Shiva in Mandsaur, Madhya Pradesh, India. It belongs to Pashupatinath tradition which is one of 6 major tradition within Shaivism. It is located on Shivna River, and is known for its eight-faced Shiva Linga. The temple sculpture is dated to the 5th or 6th century based on inscriptions, with some referring to the site as Dashapura.

topPlaces.name: Mandsaur Fort
topPlaces.description: Mandsaur Fort (also known as Dashpur Fort), is situated in Mandsaur city in Mandsaur district in Indian state of Madhya Pradesh.

topPlaces.name: Mandsaur
topPlaces.description: Mandsaur is a city and a municipality in Mandsaur district located on the border of Mewar and Malwa regions of Madhya Pradesh, a state in Central India. It is the administrative headquarters of Mandsaur District.

topPlaces.name: Sondani
topPlaces.description: Sondani, also Sondhni, is a small village at a distance of about 4 km from Mandsaur situated on Mhow-Neemuch Highway towards Neemuch in Madhya Pradesh, India.

topPlaces.name: Mandasor Pillar Inscriptions of Yasodharman
topPlaces.description: The Mandasor Pillar Inscriptions of Yashodharman are a set of Sanskrit inscriptions from early 6th-century discovered at an archaeological site at the village of Sondani (सोंधनी), about 4 kilometers south of Mandsaur (Mandasor) in northwestern Madhya Pradesh, India. These record the victory of Aulikara king Yasodharman over the Huna king Mihirakula.

topPlaces.name: Malwa Agency
topPlaces.description: Malwa Agency was an administrative section of British India's Central India Agency. The headquarters of the political agent was at Neemuch (Nimach).

items.note: Mandsaur Fort (also known as Dashpur Fort), is situated in Mandsaur city in Mandsaur district in Indian state of Madhya Pradesh.

Mandsaur is a city and a municipality in Mandsaur district located on the border of Mewar and Malwa regions of Madhya Pradesh, a state in Central India. It is the administrative headquarters of Mandsaur District.
Sondani, also Sondhni, is a small village at a distance of about 4 km from Mandsaur situated on Mhow-Neemuch Highway towards Neemuch in Madhya Pradesh, India.

itinerary.title: Day 2 — Mandasor Pillar Inscriptions of Yasodharman & around

items.note: The Mandasor Pillar Inscriptions of Yashodharman are a set of Sanskrit inscriptions from early 6th-century discovered at an archaeological site at the village of Sondani (सोंधनी), about 4 kilometers south of Mandsaur (Mandasor) in northwestern Madhya Pradesh, India. These record the victory of Aulikara king Yasodharman over the Huna king Mihirakula.
Malwa Agency was an administrative section of British India's Central India Agency. The headquarters of the political agent was at Neemuch (Nimach).

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Pashupatinath Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Pashupatinath Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Pashupatinath Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Maharana Pratap Airport, Udaipur (135 km) and the nearest railway station is Kota Junction (148 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Pashupatinath Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Pashupatinath Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Pashupatinath Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Pashupatinath Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Pashupatinath Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Mandsaur Fort, Mandsaur, Sondani, Mandasor Pillar Inscriptions of Yasodharman, Malwa Agency.
